question.text: Which of the following is the correct tax treatment for capital gains arising on redemption of Sovereign Gold Bonds at maturity?

answer.text: Capital gains are fully exempt from tax
Capital gains are taxed at 12.5% without indexation
Capital gains are taxed at 20% with indexation
Capital gains are taxed at the investor's income tax slab rate

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Capital gains arising on redemption of SGBs at maturity (after 8 years) are FULLY EXEMPT from capital gains tax. This is a unique tax advantage specific to SGBs held to maturity and is one of the key differentiators from other gold investment products. **The Trap:** 12.5% without indexation applies when SGBs are transferred/sold BEFORE maturity (and qualify as LTCG). Candidates often confuse the maturity exemption with the pre-maturity transfer taxation. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) tests taxation of gold funds which do NOT enjoy this maturity exemption. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The full capital gains exemption at maturity is the strongest selling point of SGBs over Gold ETFs and physical gold. Always emphasize this benefit to long-term investors.
